subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Quarantine and Isolation History
description: A short lesson to help you apply Quarantine and Isolation History.

hook.text: The word 'quarantine' comes from 'quaranta giorni'—40 days that ships waited outside Venice during plague.

buildup.text: Quarantine separates exposed individuals. Isolation separates confirmed sick individuals.

discovery.text: Quarantine periods are based on incubation time—the maximum days between exposure and symptom onset.

twist.text: Effective quarantine in 2003 stopped SARS with only 8,000 cases. Poor quarantine in 2020 didn't stop COVID.

climax.text: Digital contact tracing and rapid testing have modernized quarantine approaches significantly.

punchline.text: A 700-year-old strategy. Still our first defense. When it's enforced, it works.

quiz.question: What is the difference between quarantine and isolation?
options: Quarantine is for exposed people; isolation is for confirmed sick people
Quarantine is voluntary; isolation is mandatory
